DESCRIPTION: Pepsi's 'Come alive with the Pepsi Generation' translated in Chinese as 'Pepsi brings your ancestors back from the dead.' Spiritual-frame violation in market with strong ancestor veneration.
OUTCOME_SUMMARY: Campaign pulled in market; case-study staple for 60+ years.

DESCRIPTION: Swedish brand's English campaign 'Nothing sucks like an Electrolux' succeeded in UK but became meme-fodder in US English markets.
OUTCOME_SUMMARY: Brand survived; campaign quietly retired in US.

DESCRIPTION: Ford launched the Pinto in Brazil; 'pinto' is Brazilian-Portuguese slang for small male genitals. Sales were a fraction of forecast.
OUTCOME_SUMMARY: Renamed to 'Corcel' in Brazil; sales recovered.

DESCRIPTION: Nestlé marketed infant formula in African markets with insufficient clean-water access, contributing to infant deaths. Spiritual-ethical violation triggered 7-year global boycott.
OUTCOME_SUMMARY: 7-year boycott; WHO marketing code (1981) directly attributable to case.

DESCRIPTION: Coors' 'Turn it loose' slogan, when translated to Spanish, read as 'suffer from diarrhea.' Material brand reputation damage.
OUTCOME_SUMMARY: Spanish-market entry delayed; campaign re-shot.

DESCRIPTION: P&G's diaper ad showed a stork delivering babies. In Japan, stork mythology is about peach-borne babies. Cultural-narrative mismatch made ad confusing rather than offensive — sales muted.
OUTCOME_SUMMARY: Ad replaced with Japanese-mythology-aligned imagery; sales recovered.

DESCRIPTION: Coca-Cola replaced classic formula with 'New Coke.' Status-quo violation triggered consumer revolt. Reformulated within 79 days.
OUTCOME_SUMMARY: Reverted within 79 days; brand-trust narrative shifted for decade.

DESCRIPTION: KFC's signature slogan translated in Chinese as 'Eat your fingers off.' Threat-framing violation in market that prizes pragmatism.
OUTCOME_SUMMARY: Slogan dropped in market; alternative wording used.

DESCRIPTION: Mercedes-Benz entered China as 'Bensi,' which read homophonically as 'rush to die.' The brand quickly switched to 'Benchi' (meaning 'dashing speed'). Early sales were materially affected.
OUTCOME_SUMMARY: Localized name change; Chinese launch delayed and re-budgeted.

DESCRIPTION: Audi kept German tagline 'Vorsprung durch Technik' in English markets rather than translating. Counterintuitively succeeded — pragmatic German identity reinforced brand. Case study in 'don't translate.'
OUTCOME_SUMMARY: Tagline still in use 30+ years; brand-equity case study.

DESCRIPTION: P&G launched feminine-hygiene brand 'Whisper' globally but 'Always' in collectivist markets like Italy and Spain where 'whisper' framing felt isolating rather than supportive. Successful frame split.
OUTCOME_SUMMARY: Both brand names continue; deliberate cultural-frame split.

DESCRIPTION: AA's 'Fly in leather' translated to Mexican Spanish as 'Fly naked' ('Vuela en cuero' is colloquial for nudity). Premium-cabin positioning undermined in entire Mexican market.
OUTCOME_SUMMARY: Premium ad budget rewritten for Mexican market.

DESCRIPTION: 'Got Milk?' translated literally into Spanish as '¿Tienes leche?' which read in Mexico as 'Are you lactating?' Maternal-frame collision with brand intent.
OUTCOME_SUMMARY: Replaced with 'Familia, Amor y Leche' in Mexican market.

DESCRIPTION: Parker's 'It won't leak in your pocket and embarrass you' translated into Spanish using 'embarazar,' which means 'to impregnate.' The ad ran as 'It won't leak in your pocket and impregnate you.'
OUTCOME_SUMMARY: Brand mockery in Spanish-speaking markets for years.

DESCRIPTION: Levi's launched 501 with US individualist 'rebel' messaging in India. Collectivist Indian market preferred family-celebration framing. Sales muted until campaign rewritten.
OUTCOME_SUMMARY: Re-shot for family-frame in India; sales recovered.

DESCRIPTION: Renault Kangoo sold well in most markets but in Spain, 'Kangoo' sounded similar to slang for awkward person. Sales soft until Spanish-specific positioning was added.
OUTCOME_SUMMARY: Spain-specific campaign launched; sales recovered.

DESCRIPTION: Nike Air-Bakin shoe 'Air' flame-script logo resembled the Arabic word for 'Allah.' Spiritual-ethical violation in MENA market.
OUTCOME_SUMMARY: 38,000 shoes recalled; logo redesigned.

DESCRIPTION: McDonald's revealed its US fries were cooked in beef tallow despite vegetarian claims. Spiritual-ethical violation in Hindu Indian diaspora led to $10M lawsuit settlement.
OUTCOME_SUMMARY: $10M settlement; vegetarian-only India menu post-launch.

DESCRIPTION: Saudi religious police banned Barbie dolls, calling them 'a threat to morality' due to Western individualist femininity. Spiritual-ethical frame collision.
OUTCOME_SUMMARY: Banned 2003; Fulla doll dominated regional market.

DESCRIPTION: Walmart's friendliness training (smiling at strangers, in-store chants) violated German cultural norms around workplace formality and personal space. Exited Germany after $1B loss.
OUTCOME_SUMMARY: $1B loss; exit after 9 years; Harvard case study.

DESCRIPTION: Apple launched iPhone in China without an official carrier deal, leaving grey-market phones dominant. Pragmatic-frame misjudgment; consumer trust in Apple suffered for 18 months.
OUTCOME_SUMMARY: China Unicom deal 2010; market-share takeoff began 2011.

DESCRIPTION: HSBC's global 'Assume Nothing' campaign translated into multiple markets as 'Do Nothing,' implying inaction. The brand spent $10M rebranding to 'The world's local bank' to repair the damage.
OUTCOME_SUMMARY: $10M global rebrand; brand-trust impact in non-English markets for 18+ months.

DESCRIPTION: Tropicana's $35M packaging redesign abandoned the iconic orange-with-straw imagery. Sales fell 20% in 2 months. Status-quo frame violation.
OUTCOME_SUMMARY: Reverted within 60 days; $35M+ lost.

DESCRIPTION: BK Spanish ad showed tall American cowboy alongside short Mexican wrestler wearing US flag cape. Nationalist-frame and historical-grievance violations in Mexico simultaneously.
OUTCOME_SUMMARY: Ad pulled; Mexican ambassador filed complaint.

CASE_NAME: Toyota Prius Japan vs US Marketing Split
DESCRIPTION: Toyota's Prius marketing emphasized collectivist 'family' values in Japan, individualist 'eco-savvy choice' in US. Same product, opposite frame. Successful divergent execution — exemplar case study.
OUTCOME_SUMMARY: Top-selling hybrid for 15 years; case study in deliberate cultural framing.

DESCRIPTION: Gap replaced its iconic blue-box logo with a flat sans-serif. Twitter and design press revolt. Pulled within 6 days.
OUTCOME_SUMMARY: Reverted in 6 days; estimated $100M brand-equity hit.

DESCRIPTION: IKEA product names sounded similar to vulgar terms in Thai. Thai team rewrote naming for local market entry — pragmatic localization success.
OUTCOME_SUMMARY: Smooth market entry due to proactive linguistic vetting.

DESCRIPTION: Snapchat Chinese New Year filter narrowed eyes and made face yellow. Historical-grievance frame triggered in Chinese-American and Chinese markets simultaneously.
OUTCOME_SUMMARY: Filter pulled within 24 hours; public apology.

DESCRIPTION: Dove Facebook ad showed Black woman removing brown shirt to reveal white woman, intended as 'all skin types' message. Read as historical-grievance violation.
OUTCOME_SUMMARY: Pulled; public apology.

DESCRIPTION: Pepsi ad showed Kendall Jenner ending a protest by handing a police officer a Pepsi. Read as trivializing Black Lives Matter — historical-grievance and threat-framing simultaneously.
OUTCOME_SUMMARY: Pulled in 24 hours; CEO public apology.

CASE_NAME: WeWork 'Live the We Life' Global Campaign
DESCRIPTION: WeWork's collectivist-Western 'community' framing failed in individualist markets like Germany and pragmatist markets like Japan. Vacancy rates 2-3x higher in those markets.
OUTCOME_SUMMARY: Market exits; $39B → $9B valuation collapse partly attributable to GTM mismatch.

DESCRIPTION: H&M product page featured Black child model wearing hoodie labeled 'Coolest monkey in the jungle.' Historical-grievance violation.
OUTCOME_SUMMARY: Product pulled; CEO public apology.

DESCRIPTION: Heineken Light ad showed bartender sliding a beer past darker-skinned patrons to a lighter-skinned one with the tagline 'Sometimes lighter is better.' Historical-grievance frame collision in US.
OUTCOME_SUMMARY: Pulled within 48 hours; public apology.

DESCRIPTION: D&G aired a campaign showing Chinese model awkwardly eating pizza with chopsticks. Read in Chinese market as nationalist insult; leaked DMs from designer compounded historical-grievance frame.
OUTCOME_SUMMARY: Shanghai show canceled; estimated $500M+ China revenue impact over 2 years.

DESCRIPTION: BK New Zealand ad showed customers eating Whoppers with oversized chopsticks. Nationalist-frame violation in East Asian markets; circulated globally.
OUTCOME_SUMMARY: Pulled; BK NZ public apology.

DESCRIPTION: Disney's Mulan was boycotted in US for actress's pro-HK-government comments and in China for being filmed near Xinjiang. Historical-grievance frame fired in opposite directions in two markets.
OUTCOME_SUMMARY: $200M production grossed $70M; one of Disney's biggest losses of the decade.

DESCRIPTION: Balenciaga campaign featured children with BDSM-themed teddy bears and adjacent court-document props. Spiritual-ethical violation in every market simultaneously.
OUTCOME_SUMMARY: Campaign pulled; lawsuits filed; ~30% sales decline that quarter.

DESCRIPTION: Bud Light's trans-influencer partnership triggered a multi-month boycott in US conservative markets. Status-quo and historical-grievance frames collided with brand's pragmatic identity.
OUTCOME_SUMMARY: ~$400M sales decline; market-share loss to Modelo.

DESCRIPTION: Twitter rebranded to X overnight in 2023. Status-quo frame violation across all 240M+ users simultaneously. Brand equity ($24B in 2018) largely destroyed in days.
OUTCOME_SUMMARY: ~$20B brand-equity loss; advertiser exodus.
